---
title: "Jared Wightman"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: cosmo
---

## Contact

- 📧 wig20001@byui.edu  
- 🔗 https://linkedin.com/in/jaredwightman  
- 💻 https://github.com/JaredWightman  

---

## Education

**Bachelor of Science, Computer Engineering**  
*Brigham Young University – Idaho* — Rexburg, Idaho  
Expected: July 2027  

- **GPA:** 3.8  
- **Relevant Coursework:** Embedded Systems, Digital System Design, Electric Circuit Analysis II, Electric Circuit Fab & Eval Lab, Applied Programming, Machine Learning  
- **Societies:** IEEE, BYUI Computing Society, BYUI Amateur Radio Society, BYUI Interfaith Leadership Society  

---

## Skills

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Software**

- Vivado  
- Visual Studio  
- STM32CubeIDE  
- Arduino IDE  
- JetBrains IDE  
- Logisim  
- LTSpice  
- KiCad  
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**Hardware**

- Soldering  
- PCBs  
- Oscilloscope  
- Logic Analyzer  
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**Languages**

- C/C++  
- Python  
- ASM  
- Verilog  
:::
:::

---

## Projects

### Lighting Control Console  
*KiCad, STM32CubeIDE/C*  
- Collaborated on a team of 4 to create a DMX lighting console  
- Designed software for multiple inputs and outputs  
- Verified schematics and laid out a UART–RS485 daughterboard  
- Assisted with assembly and soldering  

### LED Matrix Controller  
*Vivado, Verilog, ASM*  
- Built a microprocessor simulation on a Basys3 FPGA  
- Interfaced with a HUB75 LED matrix  
- Implemented a 16-bit CPU executing RISC-style instructions  

### Chessboard  
*Unity, C#*  
- Developed an expandable chess game in a team of 5  
- Served as version control manager  

### Photoresistor Mouse  
*STM32CubeIDE/C*  
- Designed a light-based input device using an STM32 Nucleo-64  
- Controlled an LCD cursor using a photoresistor grid  

### Treble/Bass PCB  
*Altium*  
- Designed and assembled a PCB for audio signal processing  
- Integrated resistors, capacitors, and op-amp circuitry  

### Car Signal System  
*Vivado, SystemVerilog*  
- Simulated vehicle blinkers and brake lights on FPGA hardware  

### Simulated CPU  
*Logisim*  
- Built an 8-bit CPU with arithmetic operations and branching  
- Output to ASCII display  

---

## Volunteer Experience

### Missionary Service (Full-Time)  
*The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints* — Kennewick, WA  
Aug 2020 – Aug 2022  

- Worked 70+ hours/week in teaching, planning, and service  
- Communicated with diverse groups in varied situations  

### Tech Crew (Part-Time)  
*Timpanogos Community Theater* — Highland, UT  
Jun 2017 – Aug 2018  

- Operated lighting systems and stage equipment  
- Assisted with setup, teardown, and rehearsals
